title: What Destroyed the Civilisation of Easter Island?
content_html:
What destroyed the civilisation of Easter Island?

What destroyed the civilisation of Easter Island?

A.

Easter Island, or Rapu Nui as it is known locally, is home to several hundred ancient human statues – the moai. After this remote Pacific island was settled by the Polynesians, it remained isolated for centuries. All the energy and resources that went into the moai – some of which are ten metres tall and weigh over 7,000 kilos – came from the island itself. Yet when Dutch explorers landed in 1722, they met a Stone Age culture. The moai were carved with stone tools, then transported for many kilometres, without the use of animals or wheels, to massive stone platforms. The identity of the moai builders was in doubt until well into the twentieth century. Thor Heyerdahl, the Norwegian ethnographer and adventurer, thought the statues had been created by pre-Inca peoples from Peru. Bestselling Swiss author Erich von Daniken believed they were built by stranded extraterrestrials. Modern science – linguistic, archaeological and genetic evidence – has definitively proved the moai builders were Polynesians, but not how they moved their creations. Local folklore maintains that the statues walked, while researchers have tended to assume the ancestors dragged the statues somehow, using ropes and logs.

B.

When the Europeans arrived, Rapa Nui was grassland, with only a few scrawny trees. In the 1970s and 1980s, though, researchers found pollen preserved in lake sediments, which proved the island had been covered in lush palm forests for thousands of years. Only after the Polynesians arrived did those forests disappear. US scientist Jared Diamond believes that the Rapanui people – descendants of Polynesian settlers – wrecked their own environment. They had unfortunately settled on an extremely fragile island – dry, cool, and too remote to be properly fertilised by windblown volcanic ash. When the islanders cleared the forests for firewood and farming, the forests didn’t grow back. As trees became scarce and they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing, they ate birds. Soil erosion decreased their crop yields. Before Europeans arrived, the Rapanui had descended into civil war and cannibalism, he maintains. The collapse of their isolated civilisation, Diamond writes, is a ’worst-case scenario for what may lie ahead of us in our own future’.

C.

The moai, he thinks, accelerated the self-destruction. Diamond interprets them as power displays by rival chieftains who, trapped on a remote little island, lacked other ways of asserting their dominance. They competed by building ever bigger figures. Diamond thinks they laid the moai on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails, but that required both a lot of wood and a lot of people. To feed the people, even more land had to be cleared. When the wood was gone and civil war began, the islanders began toppling the moai. By the nineteenth century none were standing.

D.

Archaeologists Terry Hunt of the University of Hawaii and Carl Lipo of California State University agree that Easter Island lost its lush forests and that it was an ‘ecological catastrophe’ – but they believe the islanders themselves weren’t to blame. And the moai certainly weren’t. Archaeological excavations indicate that the Rapanui went to heroic efforts to protect the resources of their wind-lashed, infertile fields. They built thousands of circular stone windbreaks and gardened inside them, and used broken volcanic rocks to keep the soil moist. In short, Hunt and Lipo argue, the prehistoric Rapanui were pioneers of sustainable farming.

E.

Hunt and Lipo contend that moai-building was an activity that helped keep the peace between islanders. They also believe that moving the moai required few people and no wood, because they were walked upright. On that issue, Hunt and Lipo say, archaeological evidence backs up Rapanui folklore. Recent experiments indicate that as few as 18 people could, with three strong ropes and a bit of practice, easily manoeuvre a 1,000 kg moai replica a few hundred metres. The figures’ fat bellies tilted them forward, and a D-shaped base allowed handlers to roll and rock them side to side.

F.

Moreover, Hunt and Lipo are convinced that the settlers were not wholly responsible for the loss of the island’s trees. Archaeological finds of nuts from the extinct Easter Island palm show tiny grooves, made by the teeth of Polynesian rats. The rats arrived along with the settlers, and in just a few years, Hunt and Lipo calculate, they would have overrun the island. They would have prevented the reseeding of the slow-growing palm trees and thereby doomed Rapa Nui’s forest, even without the settlers’ campaign of deforestation. No doubt the rats ate birds’ eggs too. Hunt and Lipo also see no evidence that Rapanui civilisation collapsed when the palm forest did. They think its population grew rapidly and then remained more or less stable until the arrival of the Europeans, who introduced deadly diseases to which islanders had no immunity. Then in the nineteenth century slave traders decimated the population, which shrivelled to 111 people by 1877.

G.

Hunt and Lipo’s vision, therefore, is one of an island populated by peaceful and ingenious moai builders and careful stewards of the land, rather than by reckless destroyers ruining their own environment and society. ‘Rather than a case of abject failure, Rapu Nui is an unlikely story of success’, they claim. Whichever is the case, there are surely some valuable lessons which the world at large can learn from the story of Rapa Nui.

questions.question: Paragraph A
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 14–20

Reading Passage has paragraphs A–F. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.

Write the correct number, i–ix, in boxes 14–20 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph A”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn A: “Modern science - linguistic, archaeological and genetic evidence - has definitively proved the moai builders were Polynesians...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn chỉ ra rằng các ngành khoa học hiện đại đã chứng minh một cách chắc chắn (definitively proved) danh tính những người xây tượng là người Polynesia. Điều này hoàn toàn khớp với heading ii: “An undisputed answer to a question about the moai” (Một câu trả lời không còn bị tranh cãi cho câu hỏi về các bức tượng moai).

=> Đáp án: ii

headings: Evidence of innovative environment management practices
An undisputed answer to a question about the moai
The future of the moai statues
A theory which supports a local belief
Two opposing views about the Rapanui people
How the statues made a situation worse
_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.questionHtml:
List of Headings

  1. Evidence of innovative environment management practices
  2. An undisputed answer to a question about the moai
  3. The future of the moai statues
  4. A theory which supports a local belief
  5. The future of Easter Island
  6. Two opposing views about the Rapanui people
  7. Destruction outside the inhabitants’ control
  8. How the statues made a situation worse
  9. Diminishing food resources

14_______  Paragraph A

15_______  Paragraph B

16_______  Paragraph C

17_______  Paragraph D

18_______  Paragraph E

19_______  Paragraph F

questions.question: Paragraph B
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph B”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “As trees became scarce and they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing, they ate birds. Soil erosion decreased their crop yields.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Khi nguồn tài nguyên cây cối cạn kiệt, người dân không thể đóng thuyền đi đánh cá mà phải ăn thịt chim, đồng thời năng suất cây trồng bị giảm sút. Các thông tin này mô tả việc nguồn thực phẩm bị thu hẹp lại, khớp với heading ix: “Diminishing food resources” (Nguồn tài nguyên lương thực giảm dần).

=> Đáp án: ix

questions.question: Paragraph C
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph C”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn C: “The moai, he thinks, accelerated the self-destruction... Diamond thinks they laid the moai on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails, but that required both a lot of wood and a lot of people. To feed the people, even more land had to be cleared.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Theo giả thuyết của Diamond, việc chế tạo và vận chuyển các bức tượng moai đã đẩy nhanh quá trình tự hủy diệt vì nó tiêu tốn quá nhiều gỗ và đất đai. Việc này khớp với heading viii: “How the statues made a situation worse” (Cách các bức tượng làm cho tình hình tồi tệ hơn).

=> Đáp án: viii

questions.question: Paragraph D
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph D”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn D: “They built thousands of circular stone windbreaks and gardened inside them, and used broken volcanic rocks to keep the soil moist. In short, Hunt and Lipo argue, the prehistoric Rapanui were pioneers of sustainable farming.”  

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn mô tả các biện pháp nông nghiệp sáng tạo của người cổ đại như xây tường đá chắn gió, dùng đá núi lửa để giữ ẩm cho đất để canh tác bền vững. Thông tin này khớp với heading i: “Evidence of innovative environment management practices” (Bằng chứng về các hoạt động quản lý môi trường đổi mới/sáng tạo).

=> Đáp án: i

questions.question: Paragraph E
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph E”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn E: “They also believe that moving the moai required few people and no wood, because they were walked upright. On that issue, Hunt and Lipo say, archaeological evidence backs up Rapanui folklore.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Hunt và Lipo đưa ra giả thuyết rằng các bức tượng được di chuyển ở tư thế đứng thẳng. Nghiên cứu thực nghiệm này đã củng cố/ủng hộ cho câu chuyện dân gian địa phương (backs up Rapanui folklore) rằng các bức tượng biết đi. Điều này khớp với heading iv: “A theory which supports a local belief” (Một lý thuyết ủng hộ niềm tin của địa phương).

=> Đáp án: iv

questions.question: Paragraph F
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph F”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn F: “The rats arrived along with the settlers, and in just a few years... they would have overrun the island. They would have prevented the reseeding of the slow-growing palm trees and thereby doomed Rapa Nui’s forest...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn chỉ ra sự tàn phá rừng cọ phần lớn là do loài chuột Polynesia ăn hạt cây khiến rừng không thể tái sinh, cộng thêm các dịch bệnh chết người do người châu Âu mang tới. Đây đều là những yếu tố phá hủy nằm ngoài tầm kiểm soát của cư dân bản địa, khớp với heading vii: “Destruction outside the inhabitants’ control” (Sự phá hủy ngoài tầm kiểm soát của cư dân).

=> Đáp án: vii

questions.question: Paragraph G
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Paragraph G”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn G: “Hunt and Lipo’s vision, therefore, is one of an island populated by peaceful and ingenious moai builders... rather than by reckless destroyers ruining their own environment and society.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn này đúc kết và đối chiếu lại hai góc nhìn hoàn toàn trái ngược nhau về người Rapanui: một bên là những người xây dựng hòa bình, khéo léo (Hunt & Lipo) và một bên là những kẻ hủy hoại môi trường liều lĩnh (Diamond). Điều này khớp với heading vi: “Two opposing views about the Rapanui people” (Hai quan điểm trái ngược nhau về người Rapanui).

=> Đáp án: vi

questions.question: Diamond believes that the Polynesian settlers destroyed its forests, cutting down its trees for fuel and clearing land for ___________.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 21–24

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “destroyed its forests”, “cutting down trees for fuel”, “clearing land for”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “When the islanders cleared the forests for firewood and farming, the forests didn’t grow back.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “cleared the forests for firewood” đồng nghĩa với “cutting down its trees for fuel”. Mục đích tiếp theo của việc dọn đất (clearing land for) đi liền sau từ firewood trong bài đọc chính là danh từ farming.

=> Đáp án: farming

_blockConfig.noteContent: Diamond believes that the Polynesian settlers on Rapa Nui destroyed its forests, cutting down its trees for fuel and clearing land for [21] Twentieth-century discoveries of pollen prove that Rapu Nui had once been covered in palm forests, which had turned into grassland by the time the Europeans arrived on the island. When the islanders were no longer able to build the [22] they needed to go fishing, they began using the island’s [23] as a food source, according to Diamond. Diamond also claims that the moai were built to show the power of the island’s chieftains, and that the methods of transporting the statues needed not only a great number of people, but also a great deal of [24]

questions.questionHtml:
Jared Diamond’s View

Diamond believes that the Polynesian settlers on Rapa Nui destroyed its forests, cutting down its trees for fuel and clearing land for 21_______ Twentieth-century discoveries of pollen prove that Rapu Nui had once been covered in palm forests, which had turned into grassland by the time the Europeans arrived on the island. When the islanders were no longer able to build the 22_______ they needed to go fishing, they began using the island’s 23_______ as a food source, according to Diamond. Diamond also claims that the moai were built to show the power of the island’s chieftains, and that the methods of transporting the statues needed not only a great number of people, but also a great deal of 24_______

questions.question: When the islanders were no longer able to build the ___________ they needed to go fishing, they began using the island's birds as food.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “no longer able to build”, “needed to go fishing”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “As trees became scarce and they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “no longer able to build” đồng nghĩa với “could no longer construct”. Vật thể họ cần chế tạo để phục vụ mục đích đánh cá (for fishing) chính là các chiếc thuyền gỗ — canoes.

=> Đáp án: canoes

questions.question: They began using the island's ___________ as a food source.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “began using”, “as a food source” (như một nguồn thức ăn)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B: “...and they could no longer construct wooden canoes for fishing, they ate birds.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Khi không thể đi đánh cá ngoài khơi xa được nữa, họ bắt đầu chuyển sang ăn (ate) đối tượng trên đảo để làm nguồn thức ăn thay thế. Danh từ chỉ đối tượng đó là birds.

=> Đáp án: birds

questions.question: the methods of transporting the statues needed not only a great number of people, but also a great deal of __________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “methods of transporting”, “needed”, “great number of people”, “great deal of” (rất nhiều...)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn C: “Diamond thinks they laid the moai on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails, but that required both a lot of wood and a lot of people.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “required both a lot of wood and a lot of people” đồng nghĩa với “needed not only a great number of people, but also a great deal of...”. Thành phần tài nguyên còn thiếu đi kèm với con người ở đây là wood.

=> Đáp án: wood

options: A. the period when the moai were created
B. how the moai were transported
C. the impact of the moai on Rapanui society
D. how the moai were carved
E. the origins of the people who made the moai
questions.question: On what points do Hunt and Lipo disagree with Diamond?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“Hunt and Lipo”, “disagree with Diamond” (không đồng ý/bất đồng ý kiến với Diamond)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Đoạn E và Đoạn G.
Bước 3: Phân tích: * Bất đồng thứ nhất nằm ở cách thức vận chuyển tượng: Diamond nghĩ rằng tượng được đặt trên xe trượt gỗ và kéo đi (laid on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails – Đoạn C), trong khi Hunt và Lipo tin rằng các bức tượng được di chuyển ở tư thế đứng thẳng bằng dây thừng (walked upright – Đoạn E) 
-> Khác biệt về how the moai were transported (Đáp án B).
Bất đồng thứ hai nằm ở tầm ảnh hưởng của các bức tượng đối với xã hội: Diamond cho rằng các bức tượng là màn phô diễn quyền lực của các tộc trưởng đối đầu và đẩy nhanh quá trình tự hủy diệt xã hội (accelerated the self-destruction – Đoạn C), trái lại Hunt và Lipo khẳng định việc xây tượng là hoạt động giúp duy trì hòa bình giữa các bộ tộc (activity that helped keep the peace – Đoạn E) -> Khác biệt về the impact of the moai on Rapanui society (Đáp án C).
=> Đáp án: B, C

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

options: A. the period when the moai were created
B. how the moai were transported
C. the impact of the moai on Rapanui society
D. how the moai were carved
E. the origins of the people who made the moai
questions.question: On what points do Hunt and Lipo disagree with Diamond?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“Hunt and Lipo”, “disagree with Diamond” (không đồng ý/bất đồng ý kiến với Diamond)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Đoạn E và Đoạn G.
Bước 3: Phân tích: * Bất đồng thứ nhất nằm ở cách thức vận chuyển tượng: Diamond nghĩ rằng tượng được đặt trên xe trượt gỗ và kéo đi (laid on wooden sledges, hauled over log rails – Đoạn C), trong khi Hunt và Lipo tin rằng các bức tượng được di chuyển ở tư thế đứng thẳng bằng dây thừng (walked upright – Đoạn E) 
-> Khác biệt về how the moai were transported (Đáp án B).
Bất đồng thứ hai nằm ở tầm ảnh hưởng của các bức tượng đối với xã hội: Diamond cho rằng các bức tượng là màn phô diễn quyền lực của các tộc trưởng đối đầu và đẩy nhanh quá trình tự hủy diệt xã hội (accelerated the self-destruction – Đoạn C), trái lại Hunt và Lipo khẳng định việc xây tượng là hoạt động giúp duy trì hòa bình giữa các bộ tộc (activity that helped keep the peace – Đoạn E) -> Khác biệt về the impact of the moai on Rapanui society (Đáp án C).
=> Đáp án: B, C
